introSection: Jetline was a steel sit-down roller coaster located at Gröna Lund in Stockholm, Stockholm, Sweden. It was built by Zierer and BHS, and was designed by Ing.-Büro Stengel GmbH and Schwarzkopf. It operated from 1988 through 2023.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride opened in 1988.

In 1997, it was modified by German firm Maurer Söhne to have a longer and steeper drop. A tunnel was also added at the bottom of the drop.

The ride closed on 26 June 2023 following an incident.

In 2024, it was announced that Jetline would close for good.

Demolition of the ride was ongoing as of November 2025.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content:
On 25 June 2023, a train derailed causing the death of a 35 year old woman and injuring nine other passengers, some of which were children. Passengers were reportedly flung out of their seat. The roller coaster was closed for investigation.

In June 2024, the investigation concluded that replacement parts for the trains were not sufficiently strong and had not been tested sufficiently.

On 28 January 2026, Gröna Lund was sentenced to pay SEK 5.2 million for their culpability and the part manufacturer, Göteborgs Mekaniska Werkstad, SEK 1.3 million.

fullText:
Jetline was a steel sit-down roller coaster located at Gröna Lund in Stockholm, Stockholm, Sweden. It was built by Zierer and BHS, and was designed by Ing.-Büro Stengel GmbH and Schwarzkopf. It operated from 1988 through 2023.

The ride opened in 1988.

In 1997, it was modified by German firm Maurer Söhne to have a longer and steeper drop. A tunnel was also added at the bottom of the drop.

The ride closed on 26 June 2023 following an incident.

In 2024, it was announced that Jetline would close for good.

Demolition of the ride was ongoing as of November 2025.

On 25 June 2023, a train derailed causing the death of a 35 year old woman and injuring nine other passengers, some of which were children. Passengers were reportedly flung out of their seat. The roller coaster was closed for investigation.

In June 2024, the investigation concluded that replacement parts for the trains were not sufficiently strong and had not been tested sufficiently.

On 28 January 2026, Gröna Lund was sentenced to pay SEK 5.2 million for their culpability and the part manufacturer, Göteborgs Mekaniska Werkstad, SEK 1.3 million.



Blue track and supports.






 * Jetline at the park's website (Swedish)
